rules.message: A tainted-source access (Hono c.req.json/query/param/header/formData/text; Next/Express/web-standard req|request|_req.json/text/formData/arrayBuffer or .body/.query/.params/.cookies; searchParams.get/getAll; process.argv) and a dangerous sink call (eval, new Function, exec/execSync/execFile/execFileSync/spawn/spawnSync, prisma.$queryRawUnsafe/$executeRawUnsafe, dangerouslySetInnerHTML, document.write, or an .innerHTML/.outerHTML assignment) both appear in the same function/method body — sanitize the value (schema.parse/escape*/sanitize*/validate*) before it reaches the sink, or restructure so the source never flows there. THIS IS A COARSE APPROXIMATION, not real dataflow: (1) co-occurrence, not tracking — `source` and `sink` only need to appear ANYWHERE in the same function/method body, with no check that the tainted value is actually the sink's argument (a source and an unrelated sink call in the same function both flag); this can both under- and over-report relative to real per-variable taint propagation. (2) the sanitizer check (schema.parse(...)/escape*(.../sanitize*(.../validate*() clears the WHOLE function body, not just downstream of the sanitizer call — a sanitizer that runs AFTER the sink, or that cleans an unrelated variable, still suppresses the finding. (3) the source/sink vocabulary is a fixed set of regex alternatives, not a runtime-configurable one — a project-specific source or sink outside this list is not detected. Comment lines are skipped via `skip_comment_lines`. Test/e2e/fixture paths are excluded outright (deployed-surface rule). Suppress a vetted case with `// taint-ok`.
